title: The Polyglot Grove

features.name: the golden fruiting tree
features.desc-on-touch: You reach and the branch bends. The fruit in your palm is warm. When you bite, a word forms: the same idea, tasting of lemon in one language, honey in another.

features.name: the red-fruit corner
features.desc-on-touch: A corner where the trees crowded close and cross-pollinated. The berries here taste of argument and agreement at once.

features.name: the learning archway
features.desc-on-touch: Branches wove themselves into a gate. A sign in no single script hangs between them: you know what it says in every language you've ever read.

features.name: the sorting ground
features.desc-on-touch: A patch where wind has gathered fallen fruit by language, as if alphabetizing had been a natural force all along.

features.name: the silent fruiting station
features.desc-on-touch: No words here. The fruit ripens in quiet. When you listen closely, you hear every language learning to be itself through the sound of growth.

features.name: the translation pool
features.desc-on-touch: Water pooled between roots. Drop a word in any tongue and watch it shimmer into every other one the trees have learned.

inscription: the grove grows new fruit in languages the reader has forgotten they knew

file.name: Untranslatable_Words_Catalogue_Scanned.txt
file.note: Anthropologist's notes on words with no equivalent — corrupted entry: each word now contains the taste of its own untranslatability

exits.label: back through the fruiting arch toward where words first learned to grow
deeper into groves where even the trees have forgotten which language they speak
toward the coast where seawater sweetens the fruit with salt and separation
back up toward the chamber where recipes remembered eating
